rationale.R1.1: The section opens with a contribution claim (4.2 NDCG@5 SOTA) and an information-gain thesis; both must be reconstructed and verified for coherence with what is actually demonstrated.
rationale.R1.2: The concluding inference—'substantially advances the state of the art'—is drawn exclusively from two internal ablations, not independent baselines; this is a load-bearing logical gap whose validity determines whether the section's core claim holds.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative results (0.612 vs 0.570/0.575 NDCG@5, Precision@1) are cited as evidence for a SOTA claim, but the only comparators are ablations of the same model; alignment between the evidence actually provided and the claim being made is the central issue.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim ('new state of the art') implies a meaningful delta over prior independent work, yet no prior independent system is benchmarked; assessing whether the novelty framing is supported is necessary.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus on substantive technical issues (logical validity of claims, evidence-claim alignment, novelty relative to prior work), or on prose and writing quality (clarity, style, sentence-level polish)?
